title: Mathematics
subtitle: Week 2: Metric Area and Geometry Basics

slides.title: Standard Units of Area
slides.text: Let's learn about standard metric units for area. Instead of using arbitrary tiles, we use standard square centimeters and square meters to measure flat surfaces accurately. In math, we express this as: Area units = standard measurements. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. Why do we need standard units instead of using any size of tile?

examples.title: Example 1
examples.content: Review standard details: Area units = standard measurements.

slides.title: The Square Centimeter
slides.text: A square centimeter is a metric unit of area. It represents the space covered by a tiny square where each of the four sides is exactly one centimeter long. In math, we express this as: 1 sq. cm = 1 cm x 1 cm. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. Can you name three small objects you can measure in square centimeters?

examples.title: Example 2
examples.content: Review standard details: 1 sq. cm = 1 cm x 1 cm.

slides.title: Measuring in Square Centimeters
slides.text: We use square centimeters to measure small surfaces like books, index cards, or smartphones. We multiply the length and width in centimeters to find the area. In math, we express this as: Area = 6 cm x 4 cm = 24 sq. cm. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. What is the area of a photo that is 10 cm long and 8 cm wide?

examples.title: Example 3
examples.content: Review standard details: Area = 6 cm x 4 cm = 24 sq. cm.

slides.title: The Square Meter
slides.text: A square meter is a larger metric unit of area. It is the space covered by a large square where each of the four sides is exactly one meter long. In math, we express this as: 1 sq. m = 1 m x 1 m. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. Would you measure the floor of your classroom in centimeters or meters?

examples.title: Example 4
examples.content: Review standard details: 1 sq. m = 1 m x 1 m.

slides.title: Measuring in Square Meters
slides.text: We use square meters to measure larger surfaces like room floors, gardens, or school blackboards. We multiply the length and width in meters to find the area. In math, we express this as: Area = 5 m x 4 m = 20 sq. m. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. What is the area of a rectangular garden that is 6 m long and 4 m wide?

examples.title: Example 5
examples.content: Review standard details: Area = 5 m x 4 m = 20 sq. m.

slides.title: Estimating Metric Area
slides.text: We can estimate metric areas by comparing them to familiar objects. For example, a student desk is about one-half of a square meter in surface area. In math, we express this as: Estimate = close guess of area in metric units. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. How can you estimate the floor area of your bedroom without a ruler?

examples.title: Example 6
examples.content: Review standard details: Estimate = close guess of area in metric units.

slides.title: Comparing Area Units
slides.text: It is important to choose the right unit. Measuring a large room in square centimeters would give a huge number, while a notebook in square meters is too small. In math, we express this as: 1 sq. m = 10,000 sq. cm. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. Why is it better to measure a school playground in square meters?

examples.title: Example 7
examples.content: Review standard details: 1 sq. m = 10,000 sq. cm.

slides.title: Drawing a Metric Square
slides.text: Let's draw a square with a side of 3 centimeters using a ruler. We draw four equal sides of 3 cm, meeting at perfect square corners. In math, we express this as: Area = 3 cm x 3 cm = 9 sq. cm. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. What is the area of the square you just drew on your paper?

examples.title: Example 8
examples.content: Review standard details: Area = 3 cm x 3 cm = 9 sq. cm.

slides.title: Drawing a Metric Rectangle
slides.text: Let's draw a rectangle with a length of 5 centimeters and a width of 3 centimeters. We draw opposite sides equal and straight using our ruler. In math, we express this as: Area = 5 cm x 3 cm = 15 sq. cm. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. Can you calculate the area of the drawn rectangle in square centimeters?

examples.title: Example 9
examples.content: Review standard details: Area = 5 cm x 3 cm = 15 sq. cm.

slides.title: Introducing Geometry: The Point
slides.text: In geometry, a point represents an exact position or location. We draw it as a small dot and name it using a single capital letter, like Point A. In math, we express this as: Point = exact location in space. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. Can you find a point on a map showing where your school is?

examples.title: Example 10
examples.content: Review standard details: Point = exact location in space.

slides.title: Points in the Real World
slides.text: We see models of points everywhere. A tiny pin on a digital map, a dot on a paper, or a star in the night sky all represent geometric points. In math, we express this as: Dot = Point location. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. How does a point help us mark a precise location on a grid?

examples.title: Example 11
examples.content: Review standard details: Dot = Point location.

slides.title: Introducing the Line
slides.text: A geometric line is a straight path of points that extends forever in opposite directions. To show it has no end, we draw arrowheads on both ends. In math, we express this as: Line = straight path that goes on forever. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. How do the two arrowheads show that a line never stops going?

examples.title: Example 12
examples.content: Review standard details: Line = straight path that goes on forever.

slides.title: Representing Lines
slides.text: We name a line by picking two points on it, like A and B, and writing a small line symbol with two arrows above the letters, like Line AB. In math, we express this as: Line AB = straight path through A and B. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. Can you draw a straight line and label two points on it?

examples.title: Example 13
examples.content: Review standard details: Line AB = straight path through A and B.

slides.title: The Line Segment
slides.text: A line segment is a part of a line that has two fixed endpoints. It has a definite length, which means we can measure it using a metric ruler. In math, we express this as: Segment AB = path between endpoints A and B. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. Why can we measure a line segment but not a whole line?

examples.title: Example 14
examples.content: Review standard details: Segment AB = path between endpoints A and B.

slides.title: Measuring Line Segments
slides.text: We measure a line segment by placing the zero mark of our ruler at one endpoint and reading the number at the other endpoint of the segment. In math, we express this as: Length = 8 cm. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. A straight line goes on forever in opposite directions, which we show with two arrowheads. What is the length of a pencil segment when you measure it?

examples.title: Example 15
examples.content: Review standard details: Length = 8 cm.

slides.title: Introducing the Ray
slides.text: A ray is a part of a line that starts at one endpoint and extends forever in only one direction. We show this with one arrowhead at the end. In math, we express this as: Ray AB = starts at A, goes forever through B. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. How does a ray differ from a line and a line segment?

examples.title: Example 16
examples.content: Review standard details: Ray AB = starts at A, goes forever through B.

slides.title: Rays in Daily Life
slides.text: We see models of rays in our daily environment. A beam of light coming from a flashlight or sunlight shining down on Mount Matutum represents a ray. In math, we express this as: Sunbeam = model of a geometric ray. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. Why does a flashlight beam act like a ray in geometry?

examples.title: Example 17
examples.content: Review standard details: Sunbeam = model of a geometric ray.

slides.title: Comparing Segments and Rays
slides.text: Let's compare: a line segment has two endpoints and can be measured. A ray has only one endpoint and goes on forever, so it cannot be measured. In math, we express this as: Segment = 2 endpoints, Ray = 1 endpoint. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. Which of these two has a definite length that you can write down?

examples.title: Example 18
examples.content: Review standard details: Segment = 2 endpoints, Ray = 1 endpoint.

slides.title: T'nalak Weaving Patterns
slides.text: Weavers in Lake Sebu create beautiful geometric patterns. They use straight lines and points in their designs to make beautiful traditional T'nalak cloths. In math, we express this as: Geometric designs = points and segments. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. A straight line goes on forever in opposite directions, which we show with two arrowheads. Can you spot straight line segments in traditional hand-woven mats?

examples.title: Example 19
examples.content: Review standard details: Geometric designs = points and segments.

slides.title: City Layout Maps
slides.text: City planners in Koronadal City use points and lines to design maps. Points show buildings, and lines show streets that connect different places in the town. In math, we express this as: Street layout = lines and intersections. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. How do points and lines make maps easier for people to read?

examples.title: Example 20
examples.content: Review standard details: Street layout = lines and intersections.

slides.title: Fish Cage Grids in Lake Sebu
slides.text: Fishermen in Lake Sebu layout rectangular fish cages in the lake water. They measure the area in square meters to decide how many tilapia fish to raise. In math, we express this as: Area = 10 m x 10 m = 100 sq. m. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. If a fish cage is 10 meters on each side, what is area?

examples.title: Example 21
examples.content: Review standard details: Area = 10 m x 10 m = 100 sq. m.

slides.title: Drawing Parallel Paths
slides.text: We can draw two straight paths that stay the same distance apart using our ruler. These paths show us how lines can run side-by-side without crossing. In math, we express this as: Distance = constant between straight paths. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. Can you draw two straight paths that never meet on your paper?

examples.title: Example 22
examples.content: Review standard details: Distance = constant between straight paths.

slides.title: Geometry Tools in Action
slides.text: Using the right tools is important in geometry. A sharp pencil and a flat ruler help us draw clean points, straight lines, and precise line segments. In math, we express this as: Ruler + Pencil = precise geometric drawings. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. A straight line goes on forever in opposite directions, which we show with two arrowheads. Why is a flat ruler necessary when drawing geometric figures?

examples.title: Example 23
examples.content: Review standard details: Ruler + Pencil = precise geometric drawings.

slides.title: Solving Metric Area Problems
slides.text: Let's solve an area problem. A rectangular classroom floor is 8 meters long and 5 meters wide. We multiply 8 by 5 to find the floor area. In math, we express this as: Area = 8 m x 5 m = 40 sq. m. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. What is the area of a notebook that is 15 cm by 10 cm?

examples.title: Example 24
examples.content: Review standard details: Area = 8 m x 5 m = 40 sq. m.

slides.title: Summary of Week 2 Lessons
slides.text: Let's review our lessons. We learned about standard area units (sq. cm and sq. m), drawing shapes, and recognizing basic geometric elements: points, lines, segments, and rays. In math, we express this as: Area = metric space, Geometry = points and lines. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. A square centimeter (sq. cm) is a standard metric unit used for measuring small areas. A square meter (sq. m) is a larger metric unit, perfect for floors, gardens, and classrooms. We can convert between metric units by knowing that one square meter contains ten thousand square centimeters. In geometry, a point is represented by a dot and labeled with a capital letter like A or B. How do geometry and area help us describe the physical world?

examples.title: Example 25
examples.content: Review standard details: Area = metric space, Geometry = points and lines.

pages:
Exercise 1: Metric Area Problems

Calculate the area using the formula and write your answer with the correct unit (sq. cm or sq. m).

1. A rectangular tuna packing box in GenSan is 80 cm long and 50 cm wide. What is its area?
4000 sq. cm

Exercise 2: Identifying Geometry Figures

Look at the drawings and write whether it is a Point, Line, Line Segment, or Ray.

1. A straight path with arrowheads on both ends:
Line
2. A straight path with two solid endpoints:
Line Segment

Exercise 3: Geometry Applications

Write the name of the real-world model represented by each geometric concept.

1. A beam of light coming from a flashlight:
Ray

performance.title: Geometry and Area Map of a Park
performance.desc: Design a small park layout in Koronadal City on paper. Draw a rectangular playground that is 10 cm long and 8 cm wide, and calculate its area. Near the playground, draw a straight walking path (representing a line segment), a streetlamp shining light downwards (representing a ray), and a fountain marker (representing a point). Label all figures clearly, take a photo, and upload it.
labels: Correct labels for Point, Segment, and Ray
